description_long: This radiator features a long, horizontal design with multiple cylindrical pipes running parallel to each other, primarily constructed from metal. The pipes are connected by a series of brackets and fittings, with visible copper or brass-colored tubing and several black mounting fixtures. Electrical wiring is routed along the top, suggesting integration with a heating or control system. The lower section includes a prominent, ribbed cylindrical element, likely serving as the main heat-dissipating surface. The overall construction is robust, with a mix of metallic finishes and exposed mechanical joints. The design is utilitarian, suitable for industrial or urban environments where durability and efficient heat distribution are required.
description: A long, horizontal metal radiator with multiple parallel pipes, copper tubing, and black mounting fixtures, featuring a ribbed heat-dissipating section and exposed wiring.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile and protruding copper tubing and mounting brackets.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the parallel arrangement of pipes, electrical wiring, and mounting fixtures.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and visible pipe connections.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view displaying the ribbed cylindrical section and the alignment of the pipes and brackets.
attribute_pipe_connections: Two main pipe connections, one at each end, oriented horizontally with side entry; additional smaller copper tubing connections visible on the side.
attribute_fin_arrangement: horizontal panels with a ribbed cylindrical section for heat dissipation
attribute_grille_pattern: solid cylindrical ribbed section with no decorative grille
